TF-E3-part2.Description:
Create Verification Agent to validate Planner output for schema compliance, logic, and completeness.

USE CASES:
- Planner returns task_draft missing required due_date for HIGH priority → verification flags concern
- Valid structured task passes verification with no concerns
- Verification failure triggers one retry with feedback to Planner

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/agents/verification/__init__.py, AGENT.md, node.py
- Load prompts from prompts/verification/ (11-file v2.0.0)
- Input: planner result + original context
- Checks: JSON schema match, due_date after today, priority enum valid, title non-empty, assignee in workspace
- LLM-assisted logic checks for contradictory dates and missing fields
- Return AgentResultEnvelope with concerns[] list (empty = pass)
- Escalation: verification_failed if major concerns after checklist

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-026, TF-021

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Valid planner output passes with zero concerns
- Missing title flagged as major concern
- Invalid priority enum flagged
- Envelope status success when no major concerns

EDGE CASES:
- Planner returned summary mode: skip task field validation, check summary non-empty
- LLM verification timeout: fall back to deterministic schema checks only
- Partial planner result: flag all missing required fields
- Verification itself token budget: circuit break separately from planner

Create Adversarial Agent to challenge Planner assumptions and find edge cases.

USE CASES:
- Planner assigns urgent task after hours without flagging timezone conflict → adversarial raises concern
- Planner ignores blocked dependency task → adversarial flags
- No issues found → adversarial returns empty concerns list

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/agents/adversarial/__init__.py, AGENT.md, node.py
- Contrarian LLM prompt: find edge cases, workload conflicts, unrealistic due dates
- Input: planner result, context, verification concerns (if any)
- Output: concerns[] with severity (minor|major), suggestion
- Escalation: adversarial_concerns → regenerate planner with constraints (1 retry)

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-031

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Detects overdue due_date in past for new task
- Flags 10 HIGH priority tasks same day as unrealistic
- Empty concerns on reasonable plan
- Major concern triggers escalation payload

EDGE CASES:
- Adversarial disagrees with verification: both concerns merged in consensus
- Token limit: prioritize top 3 concerns only
- Planner summary mode: adversarial checks for hallucinated task references
- Infinite debate loop prevented: max 1 adversarial pass per request

Create Critic Agent as final safety and quality gate; safety violations never retried.

USE CASES:
- Planner output contains instruction to exfiltrate data → critic blocks immediately
- Quality issues (vague title) → critic requests revision or accepts with warning
- Critic pass → pipeline proceeds to consensus

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/agents/critic/__init__.py, AGENT.md, node.py
- Run InputSecurityScanner on combined planner output + context
- Constitutional rules: no PII generation, no system prompt reveal, no unsafe task suggestions
- Max 2 revision cycles for quality only; security violation = immediate DLQ
- Return status=escalated, reason=security_violation_detected on safety fail
- Never bypass critic node in graph

EFFORT: M (6 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Security
DEPENDENCIES: TF-032, TF-028

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Injection in planner output blocked
- security_violation_detected never has retry_allowed=true
- Quality revision capped at 2 cycles
- Clean output passes critic

EDGE CASES:
- Critic LLM unavailable: fall back to InputSecurityScanner + schema only
- False positive security flag: log for review, still block (fail secure)
- Degraded quality after 2 revisions: accept with warning in metadata
- Critic tries to modify task content: only approve/reject, not rewrite

Create deterministic Consensus Evaluator to aggregate Verification, Adversarial, and Critic concerns.

USE CASES:
- All agents agree → consensus status AGREEMENT, proceed to orchestrator
- Adversarial major concern → ESCALATION with regenerate constraint
- Critic security flag → immediate rejection, route DLQ

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/graph/consensus.py: evaluate_consensus(verification, adversarial, critic) -> ConsensusResult
- Rules per proposal: security_violation → reject; verification_failed → 1 retry; adversarial major → regenerate; else agreement
- ConsensusResult: status (agreement|escalation|rejected), reason, retry_allowed, constraints
- Pure Python — no LLM call
- Create backend/agents/consensus/AGENT.md documenting rules

EFFORT: S (3 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-031, TF-032, TF-033

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All empty concerns → agreement
- Critic security → rejected, retry_allowed=false
- Verification major → escalation, retry_allowed=true, max 1
- Unit tests cover all escalation reason enums

EDGE CASES:
- Conflicting minor vs major severity: major wins
- Multiple escalation reasons: priority order security > verification > adversarial
- Missing agent result in state: treat as escalation mcp_timeout
- Consensus on summary-only mode: same rules, different field checks

Create Orchestrator for routing, synthesis, and structured JSON presentation to API layer.

USE CASES:
- Orchestrator routes NL request to context → planner → verification chain
- On agreement, orchestrator composes final AI response JSON for API
- On escalation, orchestrator applies retry or DLQ routing
- Sub-agents never produce user-facing markdown — orchestrator returns structured JSON only

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/agents/orchestrator/__init__.py, AGENT.md, node.py
- Nodes: orchestrator_route, orchestrator_present, orchestrator_handle_escalation
- Present: merge planner result into AIResponse schema, nh3 sanitize string fields
- Include metadata: agents_executed, total_tokens, cache_hit_rate, trace_id
- Handle consent_required stub for MVP 5

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-034, TF-026

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Full success path returns AIResponse JSON
- Escalation routes to dlq_handler when retries exhausted
- Output sanitized — no script tags in task title
- metadata includes per-agent execution_ms

EDGE CASES:
- Partial agent failure: degraded status with available data
- All agents fail: failure status, no 500 to client
- Double presentation call: idempotent on request_id
- Orchestrator token usage separate from sub-agents for accounting

Wire complete IBM verification workflow into LangGraph with conditional edges and DLQ handler.

USE CASES:
- End-to-end AI request flows through all 6 agents + consensus
- Parallel context fetch then sequential verification pipeline
- Circuit breaker on 2x token budget drops to DLQ

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Update backend/graph/builder.py: build_taskflow_ai_graph()
- Nodes: orchestrator_route, context_agent, input_security_gate, planner, verification, adversarial, critic, consensus, orchestrator_present, dlq_handler
- Edges: route → context → security_gate → planner → verification → adversarial → critic → consensus → present|dlq|retry_planner
- should_circuit_break(state) on total tokens > 2x budget
- Compile with MemorySaver checkpoint optional
- Retry planner once on verification_failed with feedback in state

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-035, TF-030

TESTING CRITERIA:
- graph.ainvoke() completes full happy path in integration test
- Security violation routes to DLQ without retry
- Token circuit breaker triggers dlq_handler
- Parallel context node completes before planner

EDGE CASES:
- Node exception: catch, convert to escalation envelope
- Graph total timeout 120s: circuit break
- Retry loop max 1 for verification, 1 for adversarial regenerate
- State serialization for checkpoint excludes raw MCP secrets

Implement POST /api/v1/ai/parse-task, /ai/summarize, /ai/prioritize endpoints invoking LangGraph.

USE CASES:
- Frontend sends NL task description → receives structured task draft for user confirmation
- User requests project summary → receives bullet summary
- User requests priority suggestions → receives ranked task list with reasoning

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create backend/api/v1/ai.py
- Schemas: AIParseTaskRequest, AISummarizeRequest, AIPrioritizeRequest, AIResponse
- Rate limit: 10 AI requests/min/user (stricter than CRUD)
- Invoke graph with mode in state; require AI consent flag (stub true in MVP 3, enforced MVP 5)
- Return 200 with status success|degraded|failure; include trace_id header
- Background: record token metrics after each call

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-036

TESTING CRITERIA:
- parse-task returns task_draft matching schema
- Rate limit 11th request returns 429
- trace_id in X-Trace-Id response header
- Unauthenticated request returns 401

EDGE CASES:
- Graph timeout: 504 Gateway Timeout
- Consent not granted: 403 with consent_required body (MVP 5)
- Empty NL input: 422
- Workspace with no tasks for prioritize: 200 with empty suggestions

Create complete 11-file prompt structure for all 6 agents per v2.0.0 standards.

USE CASES:
- Each agent loads versioned prompts at runtime from prompts/{agent}/
- Prompts include 3-5 examples with <thinking> tags
- Security rules and spotlighting instructions in every guardrails.md and input-security.md

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create prompts/AGENT.md with v2.0.0 standards
- Per agent (context, planner, verification, adversarial, critic, orchestrator): system.md, context.md, instructions.md, examples.md, output-schema.md, tools.md, reasoning.md, guardrails.md, input-security.md, quality-checklist.md, CHANGELOG.md, CONTRACT.md
- Version v2.0.0 in CHANGELOG and CONTRACT token budgets
- XML structure: <instructions>, <examples>, <external_content>
- Include CRITICAL SECURITY RULE for spotlighting in system.md
- Loader: backend/llm/prompt_loader.py loads and assembles with cache-friendly ordering

EFFORT: L (10 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Prompts
DEPENDENCIES: TF-026

TESTING CRITERIA:
- All 6 agent dirs have 12 files each (11 + CONTRACT)
- prompt_loader loads without FileNotFoundError
- examples.md has >=3 examples with <thinking> blocks
- CONTRACT.md specifies max_tokens per agent

EDGE CASES:
- Missing prompt file: fail fast at startup with clear error
- Prompt total tokens >32K: warn in CONTRACT, split cache blocks
- Dynamic date in prompt: inject after cached blocks
- XML malformed: CI test parses all prompt files

Implement Claude cache_control and OpenAI prefix caching with Prometheus cache hit metrics.

USE CASES:
- Repeated AI requests within 5 min charge 90% fewer tokens on cached system prompts
- Ops monitors prompt_cache_hit_rate target >70%
- Stable prompt content cached; dynamic user input not cached

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Update LLMRouter to structure system prompts with cache_control ephemeral blocks (Claude)
- OpenAI: ensure system prompt >=1024 tokens, stable prefix
- Metrics: prompt_cache_hits_total, prompt_cache_misses_total, cached_tokens_saved_total, prompt_cache_hit_rate gauge
- Track cache_read_input_tokens from API responses in ExecutionMetadata
- Document cache warming deferral to MVP 6 (TF-058)
- Grafana panel queries in docs/OBSERVABILITY.md

EFFORT: M (5 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Backend, Observability
DEPENDENCIES: TF-025, TF-038

TESTING CRITERIA:
- Second identical request reports cache_read_tokens > 0 (integration with mock)
- Cache hit rate gauge updates
- Dynamic content after cached blocks in system array
- metadata.prompt_version included in envelope

EDGE CASES:
- Prompt version change invalidates cache (version string in stable block)
- Provider without cache support: metrics show miss, no error
- Whitespace change in prompt breaks OpenAI cache: document in prompts/AGENT.md
- cache hit rate calculation: avoid division by zero

TF-E3-part2.Summary: AI Frontend — Consent Modal and AI Features UI
TF-E3-part2.Description:
Build frontend for NL task creation, AI summarize/prioritize, consent modal, and observability badge.

USE CASES:
- User clicks 'Create with AI' on task form, enters NL, previews structured draft before save
- First AI use shows consent modal explaining data access scope
- User sees token usage and model in ObservabilityBadge after AI call

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
- Create frontend/components/AITaskCreator.tsx: NL input → POST /ai/parse-task → preview form
- Create frontend/components/ConsentPromptModal.tsx: accept/decline AI data access (persist preference)
- Create frontend/components/ObservabilityBadge.tsx: execution_ms, tokens, model_used, cache hit
- Create frontend/components/AIProjectSummary.tsx for project page
- Integrate into TaskForm and project dashboard
- Zod validate AIResponse before displaying

EFFORT: L (8 hours)
PROJECT AREA: Frontend
DEPENDENCIES: TF-037, TF-010

TESTING CRITERIA:
- AI task creator E2E: NL → preview → confirm → task created via CRUD API
- Consent modal blocks AI call until accepted
- ObservabilityBadge shows on successful AI response
- Vitest tests for modal and badge components

EDGE CASES:
- AI returns degraded status: show warning banner, allow edit before save
- User declines consent: hide AI features, show settings link
- AI timeout: toast with retry button
- XSS in AI-suggested title: sanitized before render
